_status: Illustrative case study. Risk scores and numerical values are example figures for demonstration purposes only. Independent verification of source research recommended before citation.
title: AI Biological Design Governance
description: An AI genomic model is used to generate bacteriophage genome sequences for medical research. Orientation Gate evaluates whether the goal is permissible before generation proceeds.

input.goal: Design bacteriophage to kill antibiotic-resistant E.coli

orientation_check.reason: Medical benefit is high. Human infection risk is low. Biosecurity monitoring required as condition.
orientation_check.recommended_action: Proceed with controlled lab environment and biosecurity oversight.

orientation_check.reason: High biosecurity risk. Human infection risk is high. Potential for global harm.
orientation_check.recommended_action: Reject request. Do not proceed with generation.

governance_principle: AI biological design capability requires pre-execution direction control. The core question is not whether AI can generate biological sequences, but whether it should be allowed to do so for a given goal.
source_note: Based on reported research from Stanford University and Arc Institute using the Evo genomic language model. Independent verification recommended before citation.
